mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals stronger real economic activity, which increases corporate earnings visibility and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of INDPRO growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 7.0% indicates tightening labor markets, which boosts consumer confidence and corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in triggered periods are negative in > 60% of cases or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption (RSAFS) and declining GDP growth (GDPC1) signals a shift from investment to consumption-driven expansion, creating positive S&P 500 momentum over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day returns in interaction periods show negative correlation with combined RSAFS/GDPC1 signals or Rank IC < 0.04 over 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 20-day lagged decline in federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) below 0.25% during high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5%) triggers central bank intervention expectations, driving S&P 500 positive returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in triggered periods are negative in > 50% of cases or interaction effect size < 0.15 in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll gains (PAYEMS) exceeding 150k in conjunction with rising unemployment (UNRATE > 8.0%) indicates labor market overheating, causing central bank rate hikes that depress S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in triggered periods are positive in > 50% of cases or interaction effect size < 0.12 in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 650k signals weak demand for construction, reducing sectoral volatility and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in triggered periods are negative in > 60% of cases or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent monthly increases in CPI (CPIAUCSL) above 3.0% during low Fed funds rate (FEDFUNDS < 0.5%) create inflationary pressures that force rate hikes, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in triggered periods are positive in > 50% of cases or interaction effect size < 0.18 in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production (INDPRO) below 95.0 combined with rising GDP (GDPC1) above 13,500 billion signals sectoral divergence, creating negative S&P 500 momentum over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in triggered periods are positive in > 50% of cases or interaction effect size < 0.14 in regression tests.
